title: auto-batch + per-instance color + perf squeeze

stories.title: **M17-batchable-color-variants** ✅ — adapter `acquireBucket({ useInstanceColor: true })` allocates the `instanceColor`…
stories.summary: **M17-batchable-color-variants** ✅ — adapter `acquireBucket({ useInstanceColor: true })` allocates the `instanceColor` InstancedBufferAttribute on the InstancedMesh + `setBucketInstanceColor(handle, index, color)` writes per-slot colour. `BatchingSystem.updateInstanced` drops `renderer.color` from the bucket key so different-coloured entities collapse into one InstancedMesh.
verification: legacy import — see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **M17-auto-batch-primitives** ✅ — `BatchingOptions.autoIncludePrimitives` plumbed through `RuntimeOptions.autoBatchPrimitives` +…
stories.summary: **M17-auto-batch-primitives** ✅ — `BatchingOptions.autoIncludePrimitives` plumbed through `RuntimeOptions.autoBatchPrimitives` + `project.json#render.batching.auto`. When on, every entity with a built-in primitive mesh, no LOD, no manifest material is auto-batched without `Batchable`. Per-entity opt-out: `Batchable: { enabled: false }`. All 5 example projects (hello-3d, beacon-world, batch-bench, physics-bench, shadows-bench) have the flag on.
verification: legacy import — see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **M17-system-ordering** ✅ — BatchingSystem moved BEFORE MeshLifecycleSystem so `BatchedMeshHandle` is set first.
stories.summary: `MeshLifecycleSystem.frameUpdate` AND `ThreeRenderer.refreshMeshes` (the fallback path called every frame from `render()`) both now skip entities with `BatchedMeshHandle` — the historical filter only looked at `Batchable`, so auto-batched entities were double-rendered (the 310-draw / 27 ms-frame regression caught during S50 development).
verification: legacy import — see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **M17-perf-ltw-cache** ✅ — `BatchingSystem.InstancedRecord.lastWorld` caches the last-written `[px,py,pz,rx,ry,rz,sx,sy,sz]` per instance.
stories.summary: `updateInstanced` skips both `setBucketInstanceTransform` AND `instanceMatrix.needsUpdate` when the LTW is bit-identical. Static buildings / rocks / roads no longer force a full 305 × 16 float GPU re-upload every frame.
verification: legacy import — see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **M17-perf-color-cache** ✅ — Same idea for `setBucketInstanceColor` — cached per-instance colour means a frame with no colour changes…
stories.summary: **M17-perf-color-cache** ✅ — Same idea for `setBucketInstanceColor` — cached per-instance colour means a frame with no colour changes doesn't dirty the instanceColor attribute.
verification: legacy import — see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **M17-perf-bucket-frustum-culling** ✅ — InstancedMesh buckets now ship with `frustumCulled = true`.
stories.summary: `recomputeBucketBoundingSphere(handle)` is called once per frame per dirty bucket (tracked by `dirtyInstancedBuckets: Set<BucketHandle>` populated by the LTW cache misses + instance add/remove). Three.js then skips the whole bucket per camera-pass when its sphere is outside the frustum.
verification: legacy import — see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **shadows-bench adoption** ✅ — `project.json#render.batching.auto: true`; trees + rocks + **buildings** repositioned to clear the road…
stories.summary: **shadows-bench adoption** ✅ — `project.json#render.batching.auto: true`; trees + rocks + **buildings** repositioned to clear the road corridors via `clearRoadCorridor(x, z, buffer)` (now per-entity buffer; buildings use `max(w, d)/2 + 0.5` so their footprint never crosses the kerb).
verification: legacy import — see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **Three.js batching research note** ✅ — `docs/research/m17-three-batching-references.md` summarises the relevant…
stories.summary: **Three.js batching research note** ✅ — `docs/research/m17-three-batching-references.md` summarises the relevant `References/three.js/examples/*.html` (`webgl_mesh_batch`, `webgl_instancing_dynamic`, `webgl_batch_lod_bvh`, etc.) and sequences the follow-up perf work into Sprint 51 candidates: BatchedMesh primary path with `perObjectFrustumCulled`, BVH extension, LOD-batched geometry.
verification: legacy import — see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

notes: Verification: `npm run typecheck` ✅; `npm run test` ✅ — 69 files, 433 tests (one existing batching test rewritten for the new colour-variant semantics); shadows-bench live probe with `batching.auto: true`: - drawCalls: **203 → 5** (40× fewer) - frame time: **5.4 ms → 1.4 ms** (4× faster) - `meshes: 0`, `buckets: 3`, `bucketInstances: 305`, `handleLeak: 0`
